title: FIRST CONTACT — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2 UPGRADED
directionRules: Every episode has a concrete objective.
Characters stay physically active and move through real spaces.
Every decision has a visible consequence.
Trust is never displayed directly; it is expressed through dialogue.
The player always receives exactly two choices.
Characters can react negatively to each other and to the player.
Every character has a distinct voice.
The story feels like a live rescue operation, not a static chat.
episodeObjective: Guide Dr. Ethan Hale from the unstable first relay contact point to the relay access room, keep him moving through real station spaces, restore the carrier, and secure the first Door 12 evidence package while establishing the player as an active remote operator.

relay_password.title: RELAY ACCESS
relay_password.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization recovered from Hale's crew log.

e01_n01.speaker: HALE
e01_n01.objective: Establish first contact and confirm there is a living operator on the other end.

events.text: [UNKNOWN RELAY SIGNAL DETECTED]
Can you hear me?
No. Of course not.
That would be too convenient, and apparently convenience was not included in this station's design philosophy.
Please don't be another automated relay echo. I have been losing arguments with machines for weeks.
Wait.
You answered.
Okay. That's real. You're real.
Sorry. I had a better opening line planned. Something calm. Professional. Reassuring.
Then an actual human being answered and my brain threw the entire plan into a fire.
I'm Dr. Ethan Hale. Chief Engineer, Orbital Research Station ECHO.
And before you ask, no, my official emergency plan was not supposed to be begging a stranger through a half-dead relay.

e01_n02_identity.speaker: HALE
e01_n02_identity.objective: Hale introduces himself while beginning movement toward the relay access corridor.

events.text: Right. Names first. Panic second.
I kept the relay spine alive after the blackout. Which sounds heroic until you remember the relay is currently coughing blood.
Chief Engineer means I was responsible for keeping things running.
Which is hilarious in retrospect.
I'm leaving the service alcove now. The relay room is two corridors down if the map is still telling the truth.
The map has been lying a lot lately.

e01_n02_verify.speaker: HALE
e01_n02_verify.objective: The player demands proof; Hale provides telemetry and starts moving.

events.text: Seriously?
No. That's fair. Annoying, but fair.
Oxygen is at thirty-two percent. Suit battery says forty, which means twenty if it's feeling honest.
My left glove has a pressure leak that I am choosing to call 'minor' because morale is a fragile animal.

events.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
events.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

events.text: There. If you're a rescue protocol, that should satisfy you.
If you're not, please pretend to be impressed.
I'm moving now. If this feed cuts out, assume I kicked something expensive.

e01_n03_moving.speaker: HALE
e01_n03_moving.objective: Guide Hale through the dark corridor toward the relay access room.

events.text: Corridor lights are dead ahead of me.
Not dim. Dead. There's a difference. Dim is dramatic. Dead is paperwork.

events.title: HALE MOVING
events.subtitle: Crossing service corridor toward relay access

events.text: I'm at the junction.
Left path is shorter. Right path goes through maintenance and probably still has emergency lights.
Short path saves oxygen. Maintenance path gives us more warning if something is waiting.

e01_n04_short_path.speaker: HALE
e01_n04_short_path.objective: Hale takes the riskier direct route.

events.text: Short path it is.
I hate that I agree with you. Oxygen math is ugly, but it is still math.
Made it.
Something scraped the wall behind me halfway through.
I did not turn around. Please appreciate my personal growth.
Relay door is ahead. Locked, because of course it is.

e01_n04_maintenance_path.speaker: HALE
e01_n04_maintenance_path.objective: Hale takes the safer but slower maintenance route.

events.text: Maintenance path. Good.
Slower, but less likely to turn me into a cautionary stain.

events.title: MAINTENANCE ROUTE
events.subtitle: Hale moves through the emergency-lit crawlway

events.text: Found something.
A crew tag wedged behind a junction box. Patel's.
He used to steal my torque wrench and deny it with religious conviction.
I hope he made it somewhere.
Relay door is ahead.

e01_n05_first_data.speaker: HALE
e01_n05_first_data.objective: Provide the first useful data before the first lock puzzle so the player has the clue.

events.text: I'm sending the access note before the panel decides to develop opinions.

events.title: CREW LOG: RELAY ACCESS
events.content:
RELAY ACCESS UPDATE

ACTIVE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
DEPRECATED FRAGMENT: 319
WARNING: Do not use full deprecated fragment.

Maintenance note: only one digit from the old fragment was retained in the active relay code.
events.caption: Recovered relay access note. This is the first usable clue for the relay code.

events.text: There. First useful thing this station has done all day.
The active code is damaged, but not useless. Starts with seventy-three. Ends with one.
Old fragment has a nine. Don't use the whole old fragment. That warning is annoyingly specific.
Panel is waiting.
I'm at the door.
It still has power. That's either good news or bait with a status light.

e01_n07_access_fail.speaker: HALE
e01_n07_access_fail.objective: Recover from a failed access attempt without stalling the game.

events.text: [ACCESS DENIED]
Wrong code. Don't panic.
Panic is how machines convince humans to make a second worse decision.
The panel noticed. Of course it noticed. It has one job and apparently it's good at being offended.
We can try again, or we can slow down and read the clue like adults. Horrible, I know.

e01_n07_code_hint.speaker: HALE
e01_n07_code_hint.objective: Give the player a clear retry path.

events.text: Look at it like a broken sentence, not a locked door.
Active record: seventy-three, blank, one.
Deprecated fragment: three-one-nine.
The system says not to use the full old fragment. It does not say every piece is useless.
So we borrow the only digit that fits and hope the relay has a forgiving personality.

e01_n08_access_granted.speaker: HALE
e01_n08_access_granted.objective: Hale enters the relay room and must restore power without a matching puzzle.

events.text: [ACCESS GRANTED]
Good.
Either you're very smart, or I accidentally taught a stranger how to break into my station.
Let's pretend it's the first one.
I'm inside.
Relay spine is starving. Life support reserve is clean. Relay feed is dirty but strong.
If we feed the relay from reserve, the signal gets clean and my oxygen gets philosophical.
If we feed the relay from the dirty node, it may scream, but I keep breathing.

choices.text: BOOST THE RELAY WITH RESERVE POWER.

e01_n09_dirty_relay_route.speaker: HALE
e01_n09_dirty_relay_route.objective: Restore the relay while preserving Hale's oxygen reserve.

events.text: Keeping reserve on life support. Thank you.
I enjoy breathing. It's one of my few remaining hobbies.

events.title: RELAY POWER ROUTE
events.subtitle: Routing unstable high draw to relay spine

events.text: Relay is stable.
Signal is ugly, but alive. That makes two of us.

e01_n09_reserve_relay_route.speaker: HALE
e01_n09_reserve_relay_route.objective: Restore a strong signal at the cost of Hale's physical condition.

events.text: Using reserve for relay. Bold.
Also personally rude to my lungs, but bold.

events.title: RELAY POWER SURGE
events.subtitle: Routing emergency reserve to relay carrier

events.text: Signal is clear.
Oxygen just dipped hard. I felt that in my teeth.
If I get lightheaded, remind me not to be heroic. I am terrible at it.

e01_n10_observer.speaker: HALE
e01_n10_observer.objective: Introduce the unknown observer while Hale continues securing records.

events.text: I'm pulling the relay logs now.
A few minutes ago I would have said this room was empty.
Now I'm not enjoying that assumption.
Don't ignore that.
But don't obey it either. There's a difference between listening and handing something the steering wheel.
I learned that late.

e01_n11_door12_direct.speaker: HALE
e01_n11_door12_direct.objective: Hale reacts to the player following the unknown signal.

events.text: Door 12?
You heard a voice from nowhere and immediately handed it the steering wheel.
Fine. If it wants Door 12, we check Door 12.
But we do it with records, not obedience.
And for the record, I am upset because I'm scared, not because you're wrong.

e01_n11_records_first.speaker: HALE
e01_n11_records_first.objective: Hale responds positively to careful evidence-first thinking.

events.text: Good. Records first. Voices second.
That is the closest thing to a survival philosophy I have left.
If the records are lying, at least they have to lie in order.
Voices don't bother with order.

e01_n12_evidence_pull.speaker: HALE
e01_n12_evidence_pull.objective: Recover Door 12 evidence and make Hale physically react.

events.text: Pulling Door 12 records.

events.title: ARCHIVE PULL
events.subtitle: Hale extracts corrupted Door 12 records from relay cache

events.title: SECURITY LOG
events.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
events.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

events.title: MEDICAL REPORT
events.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

events.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
events.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
events.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
events.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That is a lot of evidence for one very small corridor.
Manual override. Decompression risk. Medical report.
It reads like a machine trying to describe a scream.
I don't like that my outline is in the camera analysis.
I especially don't like that I can't call it wrong.

choices.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

e01_n13_press.speaker: HALE
e01_n13_press.objective: The player pressures Hale; Hale becomes defensive but continues.

events.text: I can't explain evidence I don't remember making.
And every time you say my name like a charge being read in court, it gets harder to think.
But I get it.
If I were on your side of the screen, I wouldn't trust me either.
That is not comforting. I heard it after I said it.
I'm still going to save the records. Angry or not, we need them.

e01_n13_soft.speaker: HALE
e01_n13_soft.objective: The player treats Hale carefully; Hale opens up.

events.text: Pieces.
A warning alarm. Door 12. Someone shouting my name like I had already done something unforgivable.
I remember my hand on the override handle.
Not pulling it. Not yet. Just holding it.
Then nothing.
No, not nothing.
That's too clean. This feels like a page torn out and left somewhere nearby.

e01_n14_signal_break.speaker: UNKNOWN
e01_n14_signal_break.objective: Unknown signal interrupts; player must choose between operational safety and curiosity.

events.text: Operator?
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: If you're still there, seal the records.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Not because I trust them.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Because I don't trust myself without them.
events.speaker: HALE

e01_n15_seal_records.speaker: HALE
e01_n15_seal_records.objective: Safe ending path; evidence secured and Hale continues moving.

events.title: DOOR 12 EVIDENCE PACKAGE
events.content:
Security, medical, and camera analysis records sealed.

Key unresolved points:
- Operator identity redacted.
- Hale outline detected near manual override.
- Decompression event followed immediately after access.
- Unknown signal interference present in relay carrier.
events.caption: Sealed evidence package prepared before signal degradation.

events.text: Good. Put the evidence somewhere the station can't chew on it.
And Operator? If Door 12 comes up again, don't let me dodge it.
I don't trust my memory enough to protect you from it.
I want to say I wouldn't hurt anyone.
I hate that the best I can do is say I don't remember hurting anyone.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: HE OPENED IT

e01_n15_hold_channel.speaker: UNKNOWN
e01_n15_hold_channel.objective: Risk ending path; curiosity keeps the channel open and attracts the unknown signal.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: DO NOT LET HIM CLOSE THE RECORD
You kept the channel open.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Brave, reckless, or both. Usually those die in the same corridor.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That was a joke.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No it wasn't.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHO WAS OUTSIDE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT FIRST CONTACT
We are leaving. Now.
events.speaker: HALE

operationDesign.chapterGoal: Restore first stable relay contact and extract Door 12 evidence.
operationDesign.playerRole: Remote operator directing Hale's route, risk level, power allocation, and evidence handling.
coreLoop: Hale reports his physical location and condition.
The player chooses one of two operational directions.
Hale executes the instruction in real time.
The environment reacts with risk, discovery, emotional response, or irreversible state.
Hale remembers the player's approach and adjusts his tone.

puzzlePolicy.reason: Episode 01 should teach navigation, trust, resource tradeoffs, and rescue-operation pacing before heavier puzzle systems.

persistentConsequences.impact: Episode 02 can alter Hale's tone and UNKNOWN attention based on how the player ended Episode 01.
If increased, later interruptions can arrive earlier and more aggressively.
Power and route choices can influence Hale's physical condition in later scenes.
Never displayed directly; expressed through hesitation, sarcasm, openness, or defensiveness.

focus: Concrete rescue objective from first contact to relay room
Hale physically moves through corridors and relay spaces
Player directs movement, power allocation, evidence handling, and channel risk
All visible choice sets contain exactly two options
Matching puzzle removed; only relay code puzzle remains
First clue/data arrives before the first puzzle
Failure flow retries without stalling after ACCESS DENIED
Trust is hidden and expressed through Hale's dialogue
Real-time operation pacing added via progressTask events
Choices create short-term branches and persistent route consequences
System messages reduced to functional alerts only
Hale has a distinct voice: intelligent, scared, sarcastic, guilty, and still trying to work
